free_text: Elastic Scattering of Polarized Protons from Nuclei
Near a=200 Between 11 and 14 MeV.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected
The Ta-181 Target, 10.6 mg/cm**2 Thickness and Au-197
Target, 4.9 mg/cm**2 Thickness were Obtained
Commercically As Foils.
The Bi-209 Target was 5 mg/cm**2 Thick and was
Evaporated from 99.8% Pure Bi-Metal.
The Isotopic Abundance of Ta-181 is 99.99%, Au-197 and
Bi-209 are Isotopically Pure.
The Pb-207 and Pb-208 Targets were 2.5 mg/cm**2 Thick
And were Evaporated from Isotopes of 92.4% and 99.47%
Enrichment,Respectively.
.The Optical Model Analysis.
.Two Restangular Surface-Barrier Detectors.
There are Several Contributions to the Uncertainty in
The Relative Cross Section Measurements in Addition to
Statistical Uncertainties.One Contribution is the Error
In the Cross Section Caused by the Uncertainty in the
Angle of the Detectors.The Total Uncertainty in the
Relative Cross Section Measurements Ranges from 0.5%
To 0.3%. The Uncertainties in the Polarization Range
-By Authors - Ta-181 and Au-197 have Many Low-Lying
States and Protons Scattered Inelastically to the First
Four or Five Excited States Could not Separeted from
The Elastically Scattered Protons.
In Order to Estimate the Contribution from Inelastic
Scattering, a High-Resolution Spectrum was Taken with a
Thin Au-197 Target at 13 MeV and 140 Degrees. Protons
Scattered Inelastically to All But the First Excited
State were Well Separated from the Elastic Peak.
The Analyzing Power and Yields of the Individual
Transitions were Measured. If One Assumes That the
Cross Section For Inelastic Scattering to the First
Excited State is the Same As That For the Strongest
Resolved Transition, One Concludes That the Combined
Cross Section For Inelastic Scattering to the First
Four Excited States is 2.5% of the Elastic Scattering
Cross Section at 140 Degrees. The Effect on the
Polarization Measurement is Negligible (<0.001).
For 13.8 MeV Proton Energy the Estimated Contribution
From Inelastic Scattering Increases to 4% Because 5
States are not Resolved and Because the Elastic Cross
Section Decreases,
.By CAJAD.
.The Uncertainty is Reported by Authors.
